rationales.justification: ZESCO operates the national electricity grid, a natural monopoly critical to national energy security and economic development. Private ownership without strong regulation could result in underinvestment in rural electrification.
rationales.publicInterestRationale: Universal electricity access mandate. Rural electrification cross-subsidy.
rationales.reviewOutcome: Retain full state ownership. Explore PPP for generation assets.

rationales.justification: ZCCM-IH holds government equity stakes in copper mining companies, ensuring Zambia captures resource rents from its mineral wealth.
rationales.publicInterestRationale: Mineral resource sovereignty. Dividend income for national development.
rationales.reviewOutcome: Retain majority stake. Consider listing additional shares on LuSE.

rationales.justification: ZAFFICO operates commercial forestry plantations. The commercial rationale for state ownership has weakened given private sector capacity.
rationales.publicInterestRationale: Limited. Environmental stewardship obligations can be addressed through regulation.
rationales.reviewOutcome: Proceed with privatisation. Transaction advisor appointed.

policyAlignments.measurableOutcome: Electricity access rate increased from 45.8% to 48.2% in FY2024
IDC portfolio companies contributed 8.4% of GDP in FY2024

communityServiceObligations.description: Provision of subsidised electricity connections to rural households under the Rural Electrification Programme.
communityServiceObligations.compensationMechanism: Direct budget transfer from Ministry of Energy

communityServiceObligations.description: Provision of telecommunications services to underserved rural areas at regulated tariffs.
